verdict.answer_first: A gets to the highest-risk fix immediately: "Authorization / IDOR (most serious)" and says the route "hands back any user by raw id with zero access check."
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more clearly: "The auth check (#1) and the field allowlist (#2) are the ones I'd treat as blocking" instead of mainly offering alternatives like "Either wrap... or use an async wrapper."
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on right now because its replacement route includes validation, authz, 404 handling, explicit field allowlist, and error forwarding in one snippet.
